summary: Iter9 QA of the 6-issue fix set (cascade cleanup, self-healing duplicates, DELETE company + campaign, 3-tier role system). Backend pytest 24/24 PASSED covering: (a) /api/auth/me returns role='owner' for admin@leadunify.com; seed leaves role='owner' after restart (idempotent), (b) POST /people/bulk-delete on ALL 20 seed people → cleanup.person_campaigns>0 + companies_removed>0, /api/companies total=0, /api/duplicates=[], campaigns count unchanged, (c) DELETE /people/{id} of the last person at a company auto-removes the company AND purges duplicate_flags referencing that person, (d) POST /people/delete-by-filter with company_name filter also cascades company + flags, (e) /api/duplicates self-heals a stale duplicate_flag pointing at a non-existent person_id (flag is deleted from db.duplicate_flags, does not appear in response), (f) DELETE /companies/{id} as owner unlinks N people (company_id/company_name set to null, people survive), 403 for member, 404 for unknown id, (g) DELETE /campaigns/{id} as owner deletes person_campaigns + campaign_columns + campaign, 403 for member, 404 for unknown id, (h) 3-tier role: owner can reset any user's password + change roles + delete non-owners; admin CAN invite users, reset OWN password, delete non-owner users; admin CANNOT reset other users' passwords (403 'Only the workspace owner can reset another user's password'), CANNOT change roles (403), CANNOT modify owner (403 'Only the owner can modify the owner account'), CANNOT delete owner (403 'Cannot delete the workspace owner'); role='owner' is rejected as an invite/patch value (400); member gets 403 on /users and /users/invite. Frontend Playwright verified: owner Team page shows amber 'owner' chip on the owner row with '(you)' marker, role-locked-<id> present (not role-select), disabled trash; owner Companies page shows delete-company-<id> on hover and delete-company-dialog opens; owner Campaigns page shows delete-campaign-<id> alongside Share and delete-campaign-dialog opens. Admin Team page hides reset-pwd-<memberId> and reset-pwd-<ownerId> but SHOWS reset-pwd-<self>; role-locked visible on owner row; owner trash disabled for admin. Backend seed re-ran automatically on restart (20 people + 17 companies + owner role preserved).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py grew to 2596 lines. Long-standing recommendation to split into routers (people/companies/campaigns/users) — every iteration keeps adding to it.

_cascade_cleanup_after_people_delete iterates EVERY company_id in db.companies to find orphans — O(companies) per delete. For workspaces with 10k+ companies this is expensive on single-person delete. Consider deriving affected company_ids from the deleted people docs first and only checking those.

seed_demo_data only fires when db.people.count_documents({}) == 0, but campaigns/companies leftover from previous runs are NOT reset. On restart after a bulk-delete-all, the workspace ends up with 20 fresh demo people + 17 fresh companies but ALL pre-existing user-created campaigns still there (including tests' TEST_iter9_* remnants). Consider an idempotent per-collection seed OR expose a lightweight admin 'reset demo' route.
DELETE /companies/{id} unlinks people but does not update /api/companies count invalidation clients-side — no big deal, react-query invalidates on the next page load.

PATCH /users/{id} rejects role='owner' via the 'Role must be admin or member' 400 check, but the same check is only executed if payload.role is set — an admin who omits role could theoretically set updates.role indirectly through some other path. Currently safe (only .role field triggers the branch) but worth a defensive assert.

success_rate.frontend: 100% for the 16 UX assertions run: owner amber chip + (you) + role-locked + disabled owner trash, delete-company button appears on hover + dialog opens, delete-campaign button appears + dialog opens, admin sees reset-pwd on self but NOT on other users, admin sees role-locked on owner + disabled owner trash, admin sees role-select disabled on member

test_credentials: admin@leadunify.com / admin123 (from /app/memory/test_credentials.md). Note the role is now 'owner' (auto-upgraded on startup). Test-created admin + member users use email pattern test_iter9_*_<runid>@leadunify.com / Testpass123! (cleaned up in teardown).
seed_data_creation: Test file creates disposable admin+member users via /api/users/invite (owner token), and disposable companies/campaigns via /api/import/preview + /api/import/commit (with new_campaign_name so import doesn't 400). Teardown deletes all test_iter9_* users, TEST_iter9_* companies + campaigns via pymongo. After the destructive bulk-delete-all test, teardown restarts the backend so seed_demo_data restores 20 demo people + 17 companies. Verified post-teardown: 20 people, 17 companies, owner role intact. Also manually purged residual TEST_iter9_camp_* campaigns left behind by the CSV-import seed helper (import_commit requires new_campaign_name and cannot be null).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter9 file at /app/backend/tests/test_leadunify_iter9.py. Key API contracts to remember: (1) POST /api/import/preview returns 'token' (NOT 'batch_id'). POST /api/import/commit expects payload {token, mapping, campaign_id | new_campaign_name}. mapping maps STANDARD_FIELD -> CSV_HEADER_NAME (e.g., full_name -> 'name'). If neither campaign_id nor new_campaign_name is given, 400 'Provide a campaign name'. (2) There is NO POST /api/companies for creating a company by name in isolation — companies are auto-created via CSV import when company_name is in the mapping. (3) /api/people/{pid} returns campaigns as list of {id,name} under 'campaigns'. (4) role='owner' is rejected by PATCH /users/{id} and POST /users/invite ('Role must be admin or member'). (5) Seed is idempotent — restarting backend re-adds demo people only if db.people is empty. Existing campaigns/companies from user work are NOT wiped. (6) Owner is the SEEDED admin@leadunify.com — its role gets upgraded to 'owner' on every startup if it isn't already.
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs found. All 6 user-reported issues (empty companies after delete, campaigns after delete + missing delete UI, blank duplicate profiles, missing company/campaign delete UI, 3-tier role split) are fully fixed and verified end-to-end. Minor code-quality suggestions listed in critical_code_review_comments are non-blocking.
